content5.ptxt: The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the air pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the warning light in the combination meter comes on. The tire pressure warning ECU stores a DTC when the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter stops transmitting signals. At this time, forcibly transmit the signals by releasing the tire pressure rapidly. The stored DTC is cleared when the signal transmission is resumed.

entry.ptxt: Tire pressure warning valve and transmitters stop transmitting signals

item.ptxt: Tire pressure warning valve and transmitter
It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC.
DTC C2115/15 are used only when the vehicle is equipped with a full-size spare tire.

It is necessary to perform initialization (See page ) after registration (See page ) of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or valve and transmitter has been replaced.

atten4.ptxt: Set the tire pressure to the specified value.

testgrp.testtitle: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS

test1.ptxt: Set the tire pressure to the specified value.
Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch on (IG) and turn the tester ON.
Select "TIREPRESS" by following the prompts displayed on the intelligent tester.

entry.ptxt: ID1 tire inflation pressure / min.:0 kPa (0 kgf/cm2, 0 psi), max.: 637.5 kPa (6.48 kgf / cm2, 92.2 psi)
ID2 tire inflation pressure / min.:0 kPa (0 kgf/cm2, 0 psi), max.: 637.5 kPa (6.48 kgf / cm2, 92.2 psi)
ID3 tire inflation pressure / min.:0 kPa (0 kgf/cm2, 0 psi), max.: 637.5 kPa (6.48 kgf / cm2, 92.2 psi)
ID4 tire inflation pressure / min.:0 kPa (0 kgf/cm2, 0 psi), max.: 637.5 kPa (6.48 kgf / cm2, 92.2 psi)
ID5 tire inflation pressure / min.:0 kPa (0 kgf/cm2, 0 psi), max.: 637.5 kPa (6.48 kgf / cm2, 92.2 psi)

test1.ptxt: Rapidly release the pressures from each wheel by approximately 40 kPa (0.4 kgf / cm2, 5.8 psi) /30 seconds or more.

test2.ptxt: Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the intelligent tester screen has changed.

specitem.ptxt: Each tire pressure data displayed on the intelligent tester screen will change to the value of the tire pressure.

item.ptxt: It takes about 1 minute or more to display the updated tire pressure data.

When the "TIREPRESS" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees (including the full-size spare tire). Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it.

test2.ptxt: After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the intelligent tester screen have changed, set the tire pressure to the appropriate specified values.

atten4.ptxt: If the tire pressure data displayed on the intelligent tester screen has not changed after rechecking, go to other problem procedure (For malfunction in transmission or reception function (See page )

testgrp.testtitle: INSPECT OTHER PROBLEM (MALFUNCTION IN TRANSMISSION OR RECEPTION FUNCTION)
